title: OPAL Fuels, South Jersey Industries, and Atlantic County Utilities Authority Announce Commercial Operation of RNG Facility
content:
OPAL Fuels, in partnership with South Jersey Industries (SJI) and the Atlantic County Utilities Authority (ACUA), announced the start of commercial operations at their Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) facility in Egg Harbor Township, New Jersey. Located at ACUAâ€™s landfill, the plant captures landfill gas and converts it into RNG, a renewable, low-carbon alternative to diesel and conventional natural gas. The facility, with a nameplate capacity of 2,500 SCFM, is expected to produce more than 650,000 MMBtu, equivalent to 4.6 million gallons of gasoline annually.

This marks the first project to deliver RNG into the South Jersey Gas pipeline system and the first collaboration between OPAL Fuels and SJI under their joint venture to develop RNG projects, with another facility planned in Burlington, New Jersey. Leaders from all three organizations emphasized the projectâ€™s role in reducing emissions, improving air quality, and advancing regional clean energy innovation. A ribbon-cutting ceremony is planned for October 2025 to celebrate the projectâ€™s milestone achievement.
author: Analysts Account
